conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who is 4 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To continue assessing your eligibility, can you tell me: Are you the person responsible for Alex? That is, are you the one who is primarily responsible for their day-to-day care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I'm the one who looks after Alex day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Next question: Are you or Alex currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. One more important question: Are you or your partner (if you have one) subject to immigration control, or do you have any restrictions on your right to live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any immigration restrictions. I have the right to live here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one final question to complete the assessment: Is Alex in full-time non-advanced education or approved training, or are they under 16 years old?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is only 4 years old, so yes, they're under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 4 years old (under 16)
- You are responsible for Alex's day-to-day care
- You both live in the UK
- You have no immigration restrictions

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim for Alex.

Please note: If you or your partner has an individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge, though you can still claim the benefit. You can also choose not to receive the payments but still register for Child Benefit to get National Insurance credits.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 4 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 4 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care. The claimant has no immigration restrictions and has the right to live in the UK. According to gov.uk eligibility criteria, you can get Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training) and you live in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (age 4), who lives with them in the UK. The claimant has no immigration restrictions and is the person responsible for Alex's day-to-day care. As Alex is under 16 years old and all other eligibility criteria are satisfied, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
